# Project Overview

<!-- Generated by /caveman-docs:ai-context. Manual edits overwritten on re-run. -->

## AS IS — Current state

### Purpose
Claude Code CLI plugin that enforces caveman-style compression on AI-oriented documentation.

### Business problem
- AI docs bloat with meta-prose, hedging, rationale paragraphs — burns tokens and hides facts
- Human-facing docs (README, Confluence, Jira) must stay verbose
- No existing plugin scoped to AI docs with hook-driven enforcement + generators
- Inspired by [caveman](https://github.com/JuliusBrussee/caveman); fills the AI-doc gap caveman does not cover

### Consumers and integrations

| System | Role |
|--------|------|
| Claude Code CLI | Host — loads hooks, commands, skill, agent |
| `~/.claude/settings.json` | Hook registration target (mutated atomically by installer) |
| `~/.claude/.caveman-docs-active` | Runtime flag file (created at SessionStart, removed on deactivation prompt) |
| AI doc files on disk | Write/Edit targets intercepted by PreToolUse hook |

### Macro flow
1. User runs `bash hooks/install.sh` — copies hooks, commands, skill, agent to `~/.claude`; wires 3 hook entries into `settings.json`
2. Claude Code session starts → SessionStart hook runs `caveman-docs-activate.js` → injects SKILL.md body as system context; creates flag file
3. User submits prompt → UserPromptSubmit hook scans for AI-doc-write intent → injects compression reminder if matched
4. Claude invokes Write/Edit on an AI doc path → PreToolUse hook matches path patterns → injects compression reminder
5. User invokes `/caveman-docs:init` / `/caveman-docs:ai-context` / `/caveman-docs:compress` → router delegates to `doc-compressor` agent

### Out of scope
- Writing to Confluence, Jira, Slack, GitHub Issues
- Modifying README.md, CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md
- VCS operations (staging, commit, push)
- Non-Claude-Code harnesses (Cursor/Windsurf path patterns present but not auto-wired)
